title: Novel SIMEX algorithm for autoregressive models to estimate AGN variability
abstract: The origin of the variability in accretion disks of active galactic nuclei (AGN) is still unknown, but its behavior can be characterized by modeling the time series of optical wavelength fluxes coming from the accretion disks with damped random walk (DRW) being the most popular model for this purpose. The DRW is modeled by a characteristic fluctuation amplitude and damping timescale {\tau}, with the latter being potentially related to the mass and accretion rate onto the massive black hole.
